body.title: Tickets migrate from queue.json → memories table as canonical (theigors/tickets palace echo)
body.result: Migrated 698 tickets from queue.json → clan.memories (FACTUAL rows under TICKETS_ROOT, metadata.kind=ticket). cc_queue.py now reads/writes DB as canonical; queue.json is regenerated as echo on every save. Palace pointer nodes at theigors/tickets/<id> are status-free so state transitions don't churn palace diffs. Migration script at lab/claudecode/migrate_tickets_to_palace.py (idempotent, --dry-run supported). 97 tests pass.
body.description:
queue.json becomes an echo; canonical ticket storage moves into the memory graph.

## Proposed shape
- Each ticket = memory row: memory_type=FACTUAL, parent_id=<tickets-root>, scope=class, metadata has all current ticket fields (size/tags/description/status/worker/result/decision_id/gate/...)
- <tickets-root> = new memory 'TICKETS_ROOT' under PR_IGORS_PROJECT
- theigors/tickets in palace holds pointer nodes that index into the memories rows
- cc_queue.py reshapes to read/write the memories table (via db_proxy) instead of queue.json; queue.json becomes a generated echo file updated on every close

## Migration path
1. Build the memory schema conventions first (pick parent_id, metadata keys, tag conventions)
2. Write a one-time migration script that reads current queue.json and writes 685 memory rows
3. Swap cc_queue.py to the new storage; keep queue.json write-out for back-compat
4. Verify all skills still work (sprint, ticket, fixit)
5. After a week of stable operation, queue.json becomes read-only echo

## Blocked by
- T-palace-as-source-of-truth (design decisions on storage shape)

## Not in scope
- Decisions/slates (separate tickets)
- /review or /decided rebuilds (those read the new storage when they exist)
